background: The time to failure of a component has a gamma distribution with $\alpha =3$ and $\beta=5$.

task.query: Determine the reliability of the component at 10 time-units.
task.answer: Using Eq. (2.75), we compute $$ R(10)=e^{- rac{10}{5}} \sum_{i=0}^2 \frac{\left(\frac{10}{5}\right)^i}{i!}=0.6767 $$

task.query: Determine the hazard rate of the component at 10 time-units.
task.answer: The hazard rate is given by $$ h(10)=\frac{f(10)}{R(10)}=\frac{0.054}{0.6767}=0.798 \ \text { failures/unit time } $$

task.query: Explain the other form of the gamma probability density function and its characteristics.
task.answer: The other form of the gamma probability density function can be written as follows: $$ f(t)=\frac{\beta^\alpha t^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-t \beta} \quad t>0 $$ This pdf is characterized by two parameters: shape parameter $\alpha$ and scale parameter $\beta$. When $0<\alpha<1$, the failure rate monotonically decreases; when $\alpha>1$, the failure rate monotonically increases; when $\alpha=1$, the failure rate is constant.

task.query: Provide the mean, variance, reliability, and coefficient of skewness for the gamma random variable.
task.answer: The mean, variance and reliability of the gamma random variable are, respectively $$ \begin{gathered} \ \text { Mean (MTTF) } \quad E(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta} \\ \ \text { Variance } \quad V(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta^2} \\ \ \text { Reliability } \quad R(t)=\int_t^{\infty} \frac{\beta^\alpha x^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-x \beta} d x \end{gathered} $$ The coefficient of skewness of this gamma distribution is given by $$ S_c=\frac{2}{\sqrt{\alpha}} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
